figures.caption: Table 5: Performance comparison on SWE-bench Verified. Results marked with † exclude Git hacking; for all other scores, the use of Git hacking is unspecified.

Table 1: Resolve rates (%) on SWE-bench Verified (V) and Multilingual (M). We compare the execution-free (×) paradigm against the execution-backed setup (✓) using OpenHands. Notably, our SWE-HERO-32B model is fine-tuned in two successive SFT stages using a collection of 300k execution-free and 13k execution-based Python trajectories.
Table 2: Performance comparison on the SWE-bench Verified. Results marked with † exclude Git hacking; for all other scores, the use of Git hacking is unspecified.

Figure 3: Cumulative performance and resource efficiency on SWE-bench Verified. Tasks are ordered by SWE-HERO-32b turn-count (complexity proxy). The primary axis (thick lines) shows the natural decline in resolve rate as complexity grows, while the secondary axis (faint lines) highlights the significantly lower turn-costs of SWE-ZERO models.

Figure 6: Cumulative performance and resource efficiency on SWE-bench Verified. Tasks are ordered by SWE-HERO-32b token-count (complexity proxy). The primary axis (thick lines) shows the natural decline in resolve rate as complexity grows, while the secondary axis (faint lines) highlights the significantly lower token-costs of SWE-ZERO models.
Table 3: Evaluation results of SWE-Hero LLMs under Test Time Scaling (TTS) setting using different open-sourced generative verifiers.

Figure 7: Detection of execution in the execute_bash command. This Python implementation leverages bashlex to generate an Abstract Syntax Tree (AST), ensuring all invoked commands are validated against a predefined WHITELIST.

Figure 2: Comparative overview of Agentic Workflows in OpenHands. The bottom panel depicts the SWE-Zero setup, utilizing a restricted, execution-free environment to maximize data scalability. In contrast, the top panel illustrates the SWE-Hero configuration, which transitions to a standard, feedback-driven workflow grounded in physical execution.
